metaTitle: Merge Cells in Online Table — Free | {{BRAND_NAME}}
metaDescription: Merge and unmerge cells in any direction — the feature most web table tools skip entirely. Select a range, click Merge. Free, no signup.
heroHeadline: Merge cells — the way Excel does it.
heroSubtext: Select any rectangular range, click Merge. Tablesmit handles the structure correctly across edits, sorting, and exports.

benefits.heading: Any rectangular range
benefits.body: Merge rows, columns, or both. Select the range with Shift+click, then click Merge. No limits on size or position.

benefits.heading: Fully reversible
benefits.body: Click any merged cell and click Unmerge. The cell splits back to its original structure. Content returns to the anchor cell.

benefits.heading: Exports correctly
benefits.body: Merged cells export to Excel as native Excel merges. PDF and PNG render exactly as the editor shows. No loss of structure.

benefits.heading: Right-click access
benefits.body: Right-click any cell selection to access merge and unmerge from the context menu — without using the toolbar.

steps.heading: Select the range
steps.body: Click the first cell in the range. Hold Shift and click the last cell. The full rectangular range highlights.

steps.heading: Click Merge
steps.body: Click the Merge button in the toolbar, or right-click the selection and choose Merge cells.

steps.heading: Add your content
steps.body: Type in the merged cell. The content spans the full merged area. To undo, click Unmerge — content returns to the anchor cell.

useCases: Spanning a category header across multiple related columns
Creating a row label that applies to multiple data rows
Adding a full-width note or footnote at the bottom of a table
Building complex academic tables with grouped row and column headers
